title: Enteropogon unispiceus
properties:
I can find information on Enteropogon unispiceus. 

Enteropogon unispiceus, commonly known as the one-spiked wallaby grass, has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Research has shown that extracts from the plant possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's properties may be beneficial in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. However, more studies are needed to fully understand and confirm the plant's therapeutic potential. Further research is also necessary to establish the safety and efficacy of using Enteropogon unispiceus in a clinical setting.
intro: Enteropogon unispiceus, commonly known as One-Spike Grass, is a type of grass native to Australia and Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Enteropogon unispiceus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications to take advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
